reviews.review_title: Could use a bit of work
reviews.review_description:
An interesting title but feels like it could use a bit more polish and playtesting. The hand gesture tutorial at the beginning of the game will often only show the gesture once, so if you miss it like I did, I was stuck fiddling around for 10 minutes trying to remember what gesture I was supposed to do to turn. 
The movement system also felt like it was in dire need of fine tuning. Even on a quest 3 in a well lit room, the movement crystals didn't always recognize when I was pointing at them right away, and often misread my gestures and would teleport me when I wasn't trying to. It gets frustrating when the game expects you use this movement system a lot.

I loved this game. The puzzles are sufficiently hard to make me work for the solve, but the gem of this game is the music. I felt so Zen as I was working to solve the puzzles. I felt like could drift away on the music. I how the developers bring out further chapters. Top notch and I would highly recommend.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the kind review

reviews.review_description: especially the mr is really good and fun, the levels are okay but mostly straight forward unless you have less than 0 orientation. the 4 stars are a result of the weak hand and finger tracking, although it is really fun it is also really immersion breaking when it doesn't work precisly half of the time and you're grabing thin air until the game registers

developer_response.body:
Hi Aeadraet,

Really appreciate this , great to hear you enjoyed the MR side of things.

Fair point on hand tracking too. It’s something we continue to refine, as it can definitely impact immersion when it’s not spot on.

Thanks for the feedback and for playing.

reviews.review_description: This is without a doubt the best vr mr game I've played. It's easy enough that a Newbie can get the idea of mixed reality and still enjoy the game.  Can't wait for their next one

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words, great to hear it’s working for both newcomers and experienced players 🙌

reviews.review_description: My favorite game so far on the quest; if you like puzzles you'll love it. The design is beautiful, and the way you activate the levels with a mixed reality monolith puzzle that appears in the middle of your room is really fantastic.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the positive review!

reviews.review_description:
Based on the levels I've played so far, while the game looks nice and such, the 'puzzle' element is very unsatisfying, as it consists of doing the same thing over and over again. Find some puzzle pieces using frustrating teleport movement, figure out how to construct a shape with those pieces, insert shape in plinth. Repeat. 

I thought there would be more depth to this game with all the praise it received, but it's just very samey and it means playing in short spurts to avoid boredom.


It's OK, not terrible, but if you're looking for more than a 3D jigsaw building exercise this isn't the game you need.

developer_response.body: Hi thank you for your review! We appreciate your feedback on Infinite Inside. We are looking to add more content and DLC for this game in the future and would love to hear any input on what you would like added to enhance your player experience. Feel free to reach out at support@maze-theory.com. Thank you

reviews.review_description: The game is a relatively short but incredibly fun masterpiece of full vr and hand tracking puzzle gameplay. The 3 dimensional puzzles and hand tracking really give a sense of immersion. Recommend this game for any puzzle lovers.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind review!

reviews.review_description: A mix  of puzzles and story line. I loved "Lock" but that got a bit frustrating with a few of the puzzles. "Infinite Inside" really blew me away with the look of it and the way to easily get around. The puzzles were beautiful and sufficiently challenging. Really enjoyed this one. Please give us more!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your review! It really means a lot to us. We hope to provide more in the future

reviews.review_description: A very pleasant, wonderful, and inspiring project. Thank you to the authors for letting me play it. I'm enjoying it. Great work.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the kind review!

reviews.review_title: One of the best MR games on Quest
reviews.review_description: REALLY immersive and amazing use of Mixed reality. Looking forward to Thief VR (made by the same devs). BUT where is The Lost Depths DLC, looking forward to that!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the positive review! It really means a lot to us. Whilst I can't say too much right now about the DLC, I can let you know we are working on fixing some bugs in the game and making it a better experience for everyone.

reviews.review_description:
The use of mixed reality here
 is so cool. I really enjoy the puzzles and being taken from MR to VR. Outstanding!

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the positive review!

reviews.review_description: Good mix of physical and mental puzzles. Like an escape room type mental puzzle with physical puzzles to put together.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your review!

reviews.review_description: This was a "wow" game. Great use of VR tech. Highly recommend.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review! We're glad you enjoyed your experience with Infinite Inside.

reviews.review_description: Environments are amazing, gameplay is solid, but then that’s it. Barely any story, puzzles are just rinse and repeat, no real connection. Probably better buying it on a sale

developer_response.body: Hey Ice, thanks for your review - we're glad you found the environments amazing and the gameplay solid! The story has you guided by the echoes of past explorers, you will collect shards, solve puzzles, and assemble keys to unlock the mysteries of a secret society committed to maintaining the balance of Order and Chaos. Would love to get your feedback on how we can strengthen the connection of the story and the player. Feel free to reach out to marketing@maze-theory.com

reviews.review_description: The game  was great  ! I wish they would  come up with part 2

developer_response.body: Hey, thanks so much for your review! It's fantastic to hear you thought our game was great. We are looking into additional content for Infinite Inside and would appreciate an edit on your review when new content is released.

reviews.review_description:
I overlooked this game for way too long!  The graphics are great and gameplay relaxing but challenging.  I’m so glad I didn’t wait any longer!

It’s been a month or so since I finished the game.  I am still trying to find some of the hidden keys in the epilogue so still enjoying it but I want more!  Please make another game similar to this!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the positive review!

reviews.review_description: This played fine up until I reached Chapter 3. Then the glitching started. Nothing outrageous, just annoying things that had me restart the game over and over to course correct.  After returning from searching for puzzle pieces,  the spire would appear high out of reach or  halfway sunken into the floor and unmoveble. What the hell?

developer_response.body: Hi Stinger, thanks a lot for taking the time to review our game, and we're sorry that you are experiencing these issues. We'd love to learn more about the problems you are facing and help solve them. Please can you email support@maze-theory.com with the list of glitches, and I will ask a member of the development team to look into this for you.

reviews.review_description: This puzzle world created in the style of Escher is pure magic. The movement style is perfect. I made my husband (who NEVER uses VR) try it to see what it feels like to be in a tower of blocks. He picked up the movement actions right away. This is the most unique game, story, world I’ve had the good fortune to experience. The entire game is just a work of art. The plinth that comes into your home is incredibly fun, realisticand eerily accessible. The sound and the art and  the haptics and the atmosphere are all perfection. I am anxiously awaiting more games and maybe even a sequel or maybe a game expansion?

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the positive review! We are really happy to hear you and your husband enjoy the game! The DLC is still in the works but stay tuned for more information :)

reviews.review_description:
This is a really cool puzzle game. The graphics are great as is the entire concept. There are a variety of tasks that you must complete that include putting different shapes together to be able to move forward, moving elements around on a mini version of the environment you are actually working and playing in, and much  more.

The puzzles are not too complicated but are fun and interesting. I recommend this game.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the positive review!

reviews.review_title: I'd really love to like it more.
reviews.review_description:
It's a gorgeous game. Problem is... it defaults to the narrow Meta boundary, so in the first level alone, I had to spend ten minutes trying to figure out how to get around and view the back of the pillar - because it kept disappearing when I looked at the back... and then I got a boundary warning. This happened over and over, and when I finally got to the second level, where you could do a bit of traveling... more of the disappearing.  
Combine this with no instructions, and it's a no go for me.  Too bad... because it IS gorgeous.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review! It sounds like the boundary issue may be related to your headset settings rather than the game itself. You should be able to adjust this in your headset settings to create a larger play area. We recommend checking your boundary settings to ensure they are customised to your space for the best gameplay experience. To change your boundary settings, please go to Settings (on your Meta headset) > Environment Setup. This is where you can set new boundaries and adjust the Roomscale boundary sensitivity. If however, you have tried this and the issue still persists, please email us at support@maze-theory.com and we can look into this further. Thank you

reviews.review_description: Best game played until now, relaxing ambience, very good , detalied graphics. I want to see more like this on the store.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the positive review!

reviews.review_description:
What I enjoy about this game is the puzzles and the world it take you too. You can play this in moxed reality, but I find it much more enjoyable playing in the setting the game has created.

The puzzles have you collecting pieces that you combine together to progress to the next level and its very peaceful at the same time. I enjoy playing this when I just want to think and relax.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your positive review and feedback!

reviews.review_description: Beautiful!  A true Masterpiece!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your positive review!

reviews.review_description: I have completed the first chapter, and was mesmerized.  But during chapter 2 in the labyrinth, I am holding a piece and warping, and when I warp, the hand holding the piece spins and disappears.....returning the piece back to where I found it, making me backtrack and try again.  This happened multiple times to the point of me just backing out.  Hopefully it's a glitch but it may discourage gamers if it happens a lot.  And for the record, I do very much love the look and feel of the game.

developer_response.body:
Hi gregsprinkle

We're sorry to hear about the issues you've experienced. We haven't seen these problems in our testing prior to releasing the patch so we'd love to help you get this resolved. Please reach out to us at support@saltwatergames.com, and we'll be happy to investigate further

reviews.review_description: I’ve enjoyed peacefully this game . Hope it has another edition

developer_response.body: Thank you for your positive review! I will let the team know

reviews.review_title: Fantastic Blend of Mixed Reality and VR
reviews.review_description:
Perhaps one of the best blends of Mixed Reality and VR. When the totem first ascended from my floor, I knew I was in for an intriguing puzzle game. 
Achievements added even more hrs as you hunt secret keys!

This game is stunning! I felt like I was wandering through a world created by Escher! The soothing yet haunting music only enhanced the beauty of this game. The puzzles were challenging but not frustrating. I highly recommend Infinite Inside… It’s a work of art!

developer_response.body: Praise doesn't get much higher than "a work of art"! Thanks so much for saying that GreenAcres!

reviews.review_title: Meh. No imersive narrative at all.
reviews.review_description:
There is an interesting premise here but there is no story. It seems like they tried to have some vague narrative but it's not there. The puzzles are ok but it's a trick pony. The snap turning is what frustrated me the most. Why? And the designated snap locations were too close to the environment. I love puzzle games but this is 1 puzzle idea over and over. Most of the reviews only mention the demo. Seems like they missed the full package. 

The MR is cool but it's just in certain sections. Most of the time you are wondering areas looking at set pieces with no context. Not was was advertised.

reviews.review_title: Trial was a waste of time!!
reviews.review_description: Finished both of 1st two puzzles , it stalls there !asks of i want to use pass-through I say yes, and nothing happens. Another waste of your time and money. I seriously doubt the validity of the positive feedback on this game.

developer_response.body: We're sorry to hear about this! We'd love to help you get this resolved. Please reach out to us at support@maze-theory.com, and we'll be happy to investigate further. Thank you

reviews.review_description: ￼ This game was a gift from you guys to me. Thank you. I’m looking forward to the updates that you posted. I’m excited to see extensions to this game. Keep up the great work. This is probably my favorite game to sit back and relax. That’s why I gave it five stars. ￼

A great puzzle based game. Very challenging and thought provocking.  One big issue for me is the game save. If I can't finish the chapter in one go I have to start all over again from the beginning. It's so annoying I don't know if I can keep playing

Review Update. Thanks so much for the patch!  What a huge difference.  I have changed my rating to 5 stars

10 mins and I'm hooked

11 mins onwards and I'm really enjoying it as it has a different feel to it than other games I play. Getting to grips with hand tracking is a bit annoying. But it does add to the feeling of immersion in the game.
Boring and repetitive

developer_response.body: We're sorry you found Infinite Inside repetitive. Hopefully you were able to get a refund through Meta.

reviews.review_description:
All I can say is the update makes the game more playable than ever.  Was having problems with the teleporter balls making me move twice in a row when I didn't want to.  Doesn't do that anymore!   They fixed my watch looking all gnarly on my wrist ..like it was loose and falling off. 

If you like puzzles, this is a great way to pass the time.  The Quest 3 graphics update is breathtaking.  Quest 2 graphics are decent enough as well.

reviews.review_title: Thank you for adding the save feature :)
reviews.review_description:
(Update) the developers fixed the save in game progress! They listen to their users :) 
Thank you! 
——————
I was totally enjoying this game until level 3. That’s when I discovered it won’t save progress. I made it through level 3 the second attempt. However I have had to start level 4 four times and was one one final piece away but ran out of time before I could finish.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review and updating it! I am glad we were able to make the game a better experience for you

reviews.review_description:
A lot of puzzle games I've had were boring, not this one. It's one of a kind, the graphics are amazing and the puzzles were great. Now I'm not very good at puzzles, took a while with some of them but I got there in the end. 

Couldn't stop playing until I'd completed the chapter.

Can't wait to see what the devs come up with next.

developer_response.body: Hi Alemap2108, thank you so much for the positive review! We have some stuff planned - so stay tuned for more :) Thank you!

reviews.review_description:
I enjoyed playing this game. I loved that it was relatively short, since that gives me more time to play other games. I can't speak for how the hand tracking was, since I pretty much used my controllers for the whole thing after the hand tracking felt a little sketchy at the beginning, but I normally don't use hand tracking when given the option anyway. It's really fun to be able to explore this labyrinth and see the memories of the lost people who came before. I don't know how they and all their stuff got there, but they add to the mysterious story. Maybe they just keeping throwing the suitcase to new players in hopes of escaping this time....
Some people complain about the teleporting, but it absolutely makes sense when people are expected to use hand tracking. I actually had fun getting fast at teleporting around, although it could be easier using the controllers, since you get haptic feedback from the controllers when an orb is activated. 
I did seem to get lost a lot while playing, since much of the environment looks the same, but there are landmarks that I got to know over time, and eventually I came to know the maps reasonably well. Fortunately, you can leave drawers open to show that you've already looked at a pile of stones for puzzle pieces there, in case you missed one. 
Definitely try to find the lost doors and secret keys. Those additional puzzles were pretty fun to build, since they were of regular objects and sort of wooden and less blocky than the other puzzles. If you get stuck, Google psnprofiles Any Missables? Infinite Inside.  I felt like the Workshop was only to warehouse all the puzzles, since I couldn't figure out how to travel from there to look for secret keys. I never saw the statue of the man in there, even though I watched a video that showed it there, so maybe mine was bugged. I had to just quit and go back to the main menu and restart chapters from there. 
Overall, a very neat game that I'm glad I played!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your detailed review and feedback, it really means a lot! I will make sure the develops receive it

reviews.review_description: Intriguing puzzle but the snap turns should be eliminated, so one can discover the maze with SMOOTH TURNS.

The dev asked me to tell my impressions when I had finished, so here they are:

Pros: Beautiful environments, really nice graphics, no bugs, MR works really well.  Entertaining.

Cons: I found it kind of short (you can finish it in less than 3h) and too easy.  All five chapters are essentially the same.  They just get a bit more complex.

I hope you can release a dlc with more varied puzzles.  And please allow to turn the music off.  Could be nice to have ambient sounds in addition.

developer_response.body: Hi ClassicalMusicVR, how did you find the rest of the game? We'd love to hear your thoughts!

reviews.review_description:
Wish there was more. Finished it already!

We need more!!!

Yes I’ve done all levels, and also the update. 

Need new setting, new story new challenges???

I enjoy your game, but :

After the last patch the vibration effects seem to be a bit buggy. When you first start they don't stop vibrating. Restarting (Menu & Continue level) fixes this. However, the controllers also react to teleport points outside the line of sight. Makes the game a bit uneasy. I don't remember it always being that way. It also somewhat takes away some of the fun of the game.
However, I got the game during the free period, otherwise I would regret the purchase..

reviews.review_title: Love it, but need to save progress
reviews.review_description: Love it, but if you start a chapter and don’t have time to finish you loose all of your progress when you get back to it.   Please add the ability to save when quitting…. Please.

developer_response.body:
Hi at BradWhitePanflutes,
Thanks so much for your great review! We are literally about to release an update that will allow players to save when quitting. We appreciate everyone's feedback and hear you :-)

reviews.review_title: Updated again: Until the patch arrives
reviews.review_description:
Final Review: Well, after writing a good review about their improved saved system, I've gone to log back in and my save is gone and it completely restarted me at the beginning of the game. Lol. What a joke. I advise everybody to NOT buy this game unless you plan on beating it in one go.

Updated Review: My last review was me being pretty angry with the old save system, right after realizing that it didn't save your progress in-chapter, and only took you back to the beginning. I just now played for about 30 min after reading the developer response, quit the game mid-chapter, logged back in and almost threw my headset. It didn't load up my my progress... And then it did! Haha. With nice animations I didn't expect either! Well-executed, team!

The new lighting is phenomenal too! I tripped out in passthrough for a sec because it was crazily accurate to the position of my REAL light source lol. And in the other areas it looks GREAT! WELL DONE!


Old Review: 
I won't play again until the patch arrives, and will keep the one star and not play again if the saves aren't fixed. In the meantime, if you're considering purchasing, know that if you aren't finished and you're DEEP into a chapter and you pull your headset off, you might lose that whole chapter. Extremely irritating. The game's got a lot of potential so hopefully it really gets fixed.

developer_response.body:
Hi joeMac,

We're sorry to hear about the issues you've experienced. We haven't seen these problems in our testing prior to releasing the patch so we'd love to help you get this resolved. Please reach out to us at support@saltwatergames.com, and we'll be happy to investigate further. It would be good to know what you had been doing in your play session prior to losing your save data and how you exited the level/game. 

In the meantime, I have seen that save data can sometime be lost for any game on Meta Quest devices. Do you use cloud storage? If so can you check https://secure.oculus.com/my/cloud-backup/ and see if your save data is still here? This may allow you to restore your data to your device.

I hope we can help resolve this for you.

reviews.review_description:
Cost is reasonable and I like the puzzles and teleportation system.  My main complaint is the lack of a reasonable save system.  What happens if you're in the middle of a level, maybe nearly finished, and the phone rings, your battery goes low, you need a break, etc.?  My experience is that when you come back to the game, you must start that level from the beginning.  

Maybe there is a save technique and I missed it, which is my other main complaint.  Very little tutorial information is provided.  One example:  you collect all the pieces you need to form an object, putting them into your wrist as you go along (as suggested in the early game).  Once you have them all and you're in the middle of the plinth, what to do?  You can't retrieve the pieces from your wrist.  Is it obvious that you should find the statue and pick it up in order to be transported back to the beginning?  I don't think so.

developer_response.body:
Hi NLRicker,
We've just released an update featuring a new save system, which now saves your progress whenever you collect a puzzle piece. We've also addressed several bugs and made visual improvements. We'd love to hear if this has enhanced your experience with the game. Thank you!

reviews.review_title: It's one of a kind alright
reviews.review_description: Infinite inside is so glitchy and janky it is just unplayable. The handtracking sucks, and when I try to go between it and controllers the game freaks out. The puzzle cube things are also incredibly annoying and have seizures constantly. Horrible experience.

developer_response.body:
Hi Gergity, We're sorry to hear about the issues you've experienced with hand tracking. We haven't seen these problems in our testing or received similar reports, so we'd love to help you get this resolved. Please reach out to us at support@saltwatergames.com, and we'll be happy to investigate further.

In the meantime, it might be worth checking the lighting in your environment. Playing in low-light settings can sometimes affect the tracking of hands and controllers, which could impact your experience.

Looking forward to helping you get this sorted!

reviews.review_description: This game is sooooo boring. It’s just attaching geometric shapes and that’s it. Avoid at all cost!

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your feedback. We’re sorry to hear that you didn’t enjoy the game. We understand that everyone has different preferences, and we appreciate your perspective. It would be great for us to understand what could be included into the game to make it a more enjoyable experience for you.

reviews.review_description:
For the price, this is a fascinating game.  I have never experienced anything like it, but you have to know what to expect.
It is beautiful in a very subtle, geometric way.  It is not breathtakingly beautiful like a mountain view, etc.  It is beautiful like an M.C. Escher illustration and fascinating like the movie Inception, and like both, there are worlds within worlds (hence the name).   Through it, there are small ambient elements as if you are living someone else's dream.  Old pictures and sketches and the sounds of pages casually turning in a book somewhere nearby...but you are not sure where.  You can't see it, so your imagination fills in the blanks of what is happening.  The music is dreamy and hypnotic with a touch of mystery.
The puzzles are fun but not impossible.  Exploring is fun and the teleport concept is well done, especially since I personally get motion sickness rather easily.
I am in Level 4...can't wait to see what Level 5 brings.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review MTLHED, i'll share your praise with the team!

reviews.review_description: I was hoping the first chapter was just a weak start, something better coming down the pike, then chapter 2, chapter 3 I finally bailed. Maybe I am just more comfortable with games like Myst and Riven ... but even Down The Rabbit Hole was far more entertaining than this teleporting from one port spot to the next gathering puzzle pieces without any ability to free move to explore.

reviews.review_title: The art is nice, but bleak
reviews.review_description:
When I was reading the reviews about how beautiful the game is, I guess I was expecting something like Moss, which is truly beautiful. 
Infinite Inside has so much grey, it might as well be black and white.
It would be nice to see some color, maybe a plant here and there.  Some art on the walls.
 The constant grey and brown tones are a little depressing.  
Finding the puzzle pieces is the biggest part of the game and there's no real thought or planning involved. There always seems to be 1 room that gets missed. Not because it's hard to find, but I keep going to the same rooms over and over. All the bland bricks look alike so it's hard to remember where you've already been. I don't want to be negative,  I enjoyed it so far, but I'm not excited to go back in to finish it.
Bottom line is, a slash of color would go a long way.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback Patio! The choice of colours was meant to reflect the narrative, however we take on board your suggestions! We're glad you enjoyed the game overall!

reviews.review_description: thanks for one of the most enjoyable puzzle game experiences and great atmosphere

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your review, Toxxus! It's great to hear that Infinite Inside is among your favourite puzzle games!

reviews.review_description: Where do I start? The Infinite inside is an almost indescribably beautiful  experience. I'm not going to spoil any of it for you because the less you know, the more delightful th it will be. It's no mean feat to instill a sense of child-like wonder in a 50 year old cynic. The whole thing is just so gorgeous, so delightfu,l that I am beyond impressed. The demo was itself, in my opinion, the best and most interesting thing on the Quest 3 and I have waited with some anticipation for today's release. I'm happy to say It has smashed my expectations. It's a work of art, pure and simple and an experience you are not going to find in any other game.  There is such atmosphere, attention to detail, skill and imagination here that I'm hard pressed to find anything to compare it to. Graphically it is every bit as strong as it is conceptually: A living Escher painting that has to be seen to be believed. If I could I'd live there. So good it makes me want to swear 11/10 UNMISSABLE!

developer_response.body: Thank you for such an incredibly thoughtful and glowing review! We're thrilled to hear that Infinite Inside has resonated with you on such a deep level.

reviews.review_description:
Love the puzzle play of this, and graphics are good

I must have been addicted though and played it for many hours as i completed it in 4 days, although it does say there are hidden objects to find, so still playable

developer_response.body:
Hi Kibooki, thank you for the review and praise! There are indeed some hidden rooms throughout the chapters :) 

Thank you!

reviews.review_description: I just got this app today, and I’ve played it less than 2 hours, but it won’t give me a refund

developer_response.body: Hi qqwetix, sorry that you want to get a refund. Unfortunately Meta control the refunds and not us. If you contact Meta they should be able to help you.

reviews.review_description:
Quest3  The game is fantastic. But it looses tracking quite often (hand and controlers) at that point there is no way to close the game. One has to shut down and restart the quest.

Also if you start the game outside the gaming space, it says "no tracking possible" and than one hast to shut down and restart too.

developer_response.body:
Hey ChrisKobar, thank you for reporting this! It's the first we've heard of this issue and would we love to know more. Are you playing in a dark environment?  

Would it be possible for you to email us on support@saltwatergames.com?  

Thank you, the Maze Theory team.

reviews.review_title: Stayed up too late playing it
reviews.review_description: I’m tired today. Thanks. Will probably do it again tonight….

developer_response.body: Hey Stone, how did you find the rest of the game? We'd love to read your full review!

reviews.review_description: The game Is solid. Great mix of VR and MR. In my family, 3 generations of people played and enjoyed the game!

developer_response.body: It’s wonderful to hear your thoughts on Infinite Inside! The combination of VR and MR was designed to create an engaging experience, and we’re thrilled that it resonated with you. Knowing that three generations of your family enjoyed the game makes it even more special.

reviews.review_description: Just loved this demo so good. Works really well in MR. Congratulations dev on an excellent experience. Looking forward to the full version

developer_response.body: Really glad to see you enjoyed the demo! We look forward to your review for the full game!

reviews.review_title: I was sad it didn't continue!
reviews.review_description: Amazing graphics, beautifully done. The astronomer made of stone inside (that at one point is an old man walking with a stick upside down) was so well made, so real. The way to move pointing and picking up the geometrical forms with the stars inside.... I am so looking forward to what is next!!! If this was the alpha version, I can't imagine what and how will it be the delta, epsilon or omega version. A great puzzle, a calming environment, an impressive design. I take my hat off for the game. I applaud the designers. Please, More!!!!

I really liked the game. 
I hadn’t had any bugs, it was running smoothly.
I’m looking forward to the whole game.

update
The whole game is here and I really love it. It is a lot of fun to play it, I love the concept of the game. Thank you very much for this super good game, it is really fun.
I hope there will be a sequel :-)

developer_response.body: Thanks Frezo! We're thrilled to hear you've not encountered any issues whilst playing!

reviews.review_description: Quiet puzzle game, movement with teleport, well integrated into the space. I like it very much and I'll be happy if there are more levels .-)

developer_response.body: Many thanks for the review, Sam! Upon the full release of the game, there will certainly be more levels to enjoy!
